title: The "Harness" Problem: Why Cloud AI Integrations Are Your Weakest Link
summary: When the wrapper around cloud AI breaks, the model becomes useless in your workflow — why local stacks offer real reliability over SaaS integrations.

title: The Frontier Price War: Why Open Source Is Already the Smarter Play
summary: How frontier model pricing converged at premium levels in 2025-2026, and why open-source models like Cursor's Composer 2.5 are delivering near-frontier performance at a fraction of the cost.

title: The Human-in-the-Loop Advantage & Why I Don't Trust Fully Autonomous AI
summary: Why the best AI systems keep humans in the decision loop — covering agent unpredictability, vibe coding vs agentic engineering, and why guardrails matter more than full autonomy.

title: Lean Purpose-Built AI Solutions Beat Bloated Platforms
summary: Why the flood of AI tools in 2026 is making simplicity more valuable — and why designing good process fundamentals matters more than adding features.

title: The Open Source Advantage: Why Control Beats Convenience
summary: Why I run my entire tech stack on open source — from file storage to AI development — and how control, privacy, and AI-assisted development are changing what is achievable for both hobbyists and businesses.

title: What Problem Are You Trying to Solve with AI?
summary: Dan Martell's GAIN Matrix framework for thinking about where AI actually fits in your business — and why solving real problems matters more than chasing tools.

title: Consumer or Creator? Where I Stand on AI Right Now
summary: From Microsoft Recall skeptic to local AI enthusiast — how my journey from consumer to creator with AI changed everything, and why that shift matters more than you might think.

title: Dense vs Mixture-of-Experts: Why I Switched to a Dual-Model Setup
summary: How running a dense 27B model alongside a faster 35B MoE model on my Mac Studio solved context-completion issues in Hermes Agent — and why open-source local models are getting production-ready.

title: The Value of AI Is Leverage, Not Replacement
summary: A reflection on why AI's strongest value is not removing people from the loop, but giving them more leverage for work that still requires intent, taste, judgment, and trust.
